---
title: "Changelog"
subtitle: "Release history and notable changes"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

All notable changes to `nemascan-sims-nf` are recorded here. The project does
not use version tags; entries are organized by development phase and date.


## [Unreleased] — March 2026

### Fixed
- **Missing `versions.yml` in DB migration modules** (`fix/versions_yml_db_migration`, 2026-03-05):
  Added `versions.yml` output to all 7 `modules/db_migration/*/main.nf` processes
  (`write_marker_set`, `write_genotype_matrix`, `write_gwa_to_db`, `write_trait_data`,
  `aggregate_metadata`, `analyze_qtl`, `assess_sims`). The pattern was established across
  all other module families (bcftools, GCTA, PLINK, R, Python) but had not been carried
  forward when the DB migration layer was added. All 7 processes now capture the R version
  and wire their `versions` output channel into `ch_versions` in `main.nf`.

### Fixed
<<<<<<< HEAD
- **Pipeline configuration gaps** (`fix/pipeline_configuration_gaps`, 2026-03-05):
  - Added `--output_dir` parameter for stable, deterministic output directory naming.
    The date-based default (`Analysis_Results-{date}`) is preserved when `--output_dir`
    is not specified.
  - Added `nextflow_schema.json` — machine-readable parameter schema covering all pipeline
    parameters, compatible with nf-core tooling and IDE parameter validation.
  - Documented previously undocumented parameters `--alpha` and `--legacy_assess` in the
    README parameters table and `--help` output.
  - Parameterized hardcoded Rockfish cluster paths in `conf/rockfish.config`: `--slurm_account`
    (replaces hardcoded `eande106` in `clusterOptions`) and `--scratch_dir` (replaces hardcoded
    `/scratch4/eande106` in `workflow.workDir`). All Rockfish infrastructure params are now
    overridable without editing the config file.
=======
- **Stochastic script reproducibility** (`fix/rand-seed-stochastic-scripts`, 2026-03-05): causal variant
  selection and effect size simulation are now fully reproducible across pipeline runs:
  - `bin/create_causal_vars.py` seeds `np.random.default_rng(rep)` at startup; all stochastic calls
    (`rng.choice`, `rng.gamma`, `rng.uniform`) use the seeded Generator. Previously, `np.random.default_rng()`
    was called without a seed, making results non-deterministic.
  - `bin/Create_Causal_QTLs.R` calls `set.seed(as.integer(rep))` before any stochastic draws (`rgamma`,
    `sample`, `runif`).
  - Both Nextflow modules (`PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL`, `R_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL`,
    `R_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_LOCAL`) now pass `${rep}` as the seed argument to the scripts.
  - `trait_metadata_schema()` gains a new `sim_seed` (int32) field; `write_trait_metadata()` stores the
    seed used for each simulated trait, enabling exact post-hoc reproduction without the Nextflow cache.
>>>>>>> origin/reproducibility-updates

### Fixed
- **Pipeline configuration gaps** (`fix/pipeline_configuration_gaps`, 2026-03-05):
  - Added `--output_dir` parameter for stable, deterministic output directory naming.
    The date-based default (`Analysis_Results-{date}`) is preserved when `--output_dir`
    is not specified.
  - Added `nextflow_schema.json` — machine-readable parameter schema covering all pipeline
    parameters, compatible with nf-core tooling and IDE parameter validation.
  - Documented previously undocumented parameters `--alpha` and `--legacy_assess` in the
    README parameters table and `--help` output.
  - Parameterized hardcoded Rockfish cluster paths in `conf/rockfish.config`: `--slurm_account`
    (replaces hardcoded `eande106` in `clusterOptions`) and `--scratch_dir` (replaces hardcoded
    `/scratch4/eande106` in `workflow.workDir`). All Rockfish infrastructure params are now
    overridable without editing the config file.

### Fixed
- **Stochastic script reproducibility** (`fix/rand-seed-stochastic-scripts`, 2026-03-05): causal variant
  selection and effect size simulation are now fully reproducible across pipeline runs:
  - `bin/create_causal_vars.py` seeds `np.random.default_rng(rep)` at startup; all stochastic calls
    (`rng.choice`, `rng.gamma`, `rng.uniform`) use the seeded Generator. Previously, `np.random.default_rng()`
    was called without a seed, making results non-deterministic.
  - `bin/Create_Causal_QTLs.R` calls `set.seed(as.integer(rep))` before any stochastic draws (`rgamma`,
    `sample`, `runif`).
  - Both Nextflow modules (`PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL`, `R_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL`,
    `R_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_LOCAL`) now pass `${rep}` as the seed argument to the scripts.
  - `trait_metadata_schema()` gains a new `sim_seed` (int32) field; `write_trait_metadata()` stores the
    seed used for each simulated trait, enabling exact post-hoc reproduction without the Nextflow cache.

### Added
- **Non-marker causal variants** (`feature/add-nonmarker-cvs`, 2026-03-03): causal variants
  can now be drawn from a separately-configured variant pool rather than the marker SNP set.
  Key changes:
  - Strainfile format extended to 6 columns: `group`, `species`, `vcf`, `ms_maf`, `ms_ld`,
    `strains`. Species, VCF source, and marker-set thresholds are now per-group in the
    strainfile; `--vcf`, `--maf`, `--species`, `--vcf_release_id`, `--ms_maf`, and `--ms_ld`
    CLI parameters removed.
  - New CLI parameters `--cv_maf` (default: each group's `ms_maf`) and `--cv_ld` (default:
    0.8) configure the causal variant pool MAF and LD thresholds independently of the marker
    SNP set.
  - Two parallel PLINK runs per group: `PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF` (marker SNP set, drives GWA)
    and `PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF` (CV pool, drives causal variant selection). Mixed-species runs
    supported in a single pipeline invocation.
  - `PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL` now selects causal variants from `CV_TO_SIMS.bim`
    (CV pool BIM); `GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES` now uses `--bfile CV_TO_SIMS`.
  - Marker set ID upgraded to v=2: SHA-256 now encodes `population`, `maf`, `species`,
    `vcf_release_id`, and `ms_ld`. CV parameters (`cv_maf`, `cv_ld`) are intentionally
    excluded from the hash.
  - `marker_set_metadata.parquet` gains three new columns: `species` (utf8),
    `vcf_release_id` (utf8), `ms_ld` (float64).
  - DB write scripts (`write_marker_set.R`, `write_genotype_matrix.R`, `write_gwa_to_db.R`)
    and their Nextflow modules updated to accept and pass `--species`, `--vcf_release_id`,
    `--ms_ld`.
  - `aggregate_metadata.R` fallback path updated to emit `NA` for the three new columns
    when no metadata sidecars exist.

### Fixed
- **Python dependency pinning** (`fix/python-requirements`, 2026-03-05): added
  `requirements.txt` at the repository root pinning `numpy==2.2.3` and
  `pandas==2.2.3` to the versions present in the `andersenlab/numpy:20250506`
  container. The `PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL` `versions.yml` emission now
  also records `numpy` and `pandas` library versions alongside the Python
  interpreter version, providing runtime provenance in pipeline output logs.

### Added
- **R package lockfile** (`fix/r_package_lockfile`, 2026-03-05): `renv.lock`
  committed at the project root records the exact versions of all R packages
  installed in the `andersenlab/r_packages:20250519` container. Provides a
  verifiable record of the software environment that produced simulation results
  and enables local environment reproduction via `renv::restore()`.
  `scripts/generate_renv_lock.sh` documents how to regenerate the lockfile when
  the container changes.

### Added
- **Strainfile provenance in marker set metadata** (`fix/strain-file-not-hashed`, 2026-03-05):
  `marker_set_metadata.parquet` now records two new columns for reproducibility auditing:
  - `strainfile_hash` (`utf8`) — SHA-256 hex digest of the complete strainfile, computed
    once at pipeline submission time (same value for all population groups in a run).
  - `strain_list` (`utf8`) — Comma-separated list of strain names used for the specific
    population group, derived from the strainfile at submission time.
  - Both columns are optional/NA-safe: queries against databases produced before this
    change return `NULL` without error.
  - Enables post-hoc verification that a database was produced with a particular strain
    set, and that the strainfile has not been modified between runs.

### Added
- `mappings_metadata.parquet` sidecar file written to the database root by
  `DB_MIGRATION_AGGREGATE_METADATA` — provides a single queryable index of all
  mapping runs without scanning every Hive partition. (2026-03-02)


## Phase 5 — Database Integration

### PR #98 — Hash-based IDs (2026-03-01)

Replaced sequential or filename-derived identifiers with deterministic 12-character
MD5 hex IDs, making all IDs stable across `-resume` runs.

#### Added
- `generate_trait_id(group, maf, nqtl, effect, rep, h2)` — MD5 hash of
  concatenated parameters; used as the primary key for all per-trait Parquet
  files (`traits/`, `causal_variants/`, `phenotypes/`).
- `generate_mapping_id(trait_id, mode, type)` — MD5 hash used as the Hive
  partition key under `mappings/population={pop}/mapping_id={id}/`.
- `generate_marker_set_id(population, maf, species, vcf_release_id, ms_ld)` — SHA-256 hash (v=2) written to
  `marker_set_metadata.parquet` and used in the marker-set file names.
- Unit tests for all three hash functions in `test-hash_generation.R`, with
  golden-value assertions to catch silent hash drift.

#### Changed
- Mappings Parquet schema reduced to core columns; accessible metadata
  (population, MAF, effect, nQTL, h2, replicate) is no longer duplicated in
  the mappings table — it is retrievable via the trait ID foreign key.
- Allele frequency (`AF`) column removed from the marker-set schema.
- Algorithm names standardized to `loco` / `inbred` across all pipeline paths
  (both DB and legacy) to replace inconsistent earlier labels.
- Trait storage tests updated to cover hash-based ID determinism and
  round-trip write/read.

#### Fixed
- Query functions updated to remove the defunct `AF` join that referenced a
  column no longer in the schema.
- Trait `id` generation corrected to use the hash framework (previously
  still used a non-hash method after the schema change).


### PR #97 — Automated Cross-Validation Tests (2026-02-27)

#### Added
- `test-assessment_cross_validation.R` — compares `db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv`
  (DB path) against `simulation_assessment_results.tsv` (legacy path), computing
  per-replicate detection concordance, designation concordance, and interval
  overlap (Jaccard score).
- `test-cross_validation.R` — round-trip check comparing Parquet DB contents
  against raw `.fastGWA`/`.mlma` files in the Nextflow work directory.


### PR #96 — Phase 5: Genotype & Trait Storage (2026-02-21)

Extended the Parquet database to capture the full data needed for offline
variance-explained estimation and richer downstream queries.

#### Added
- **Genotype matrix storage** — `write_genotype_matrix()` / `read_genotype_matrix()`
  in `R/database.R`; long-format Parquet files (`{pop}_{maf}_genotypes.parquet`)
  written to `marker_sets/`.
- **`DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX`** Nextflow module (`modules/db_migration/write_genotype_matrix/`).
- **Trait data storage** — three write functions (`write_trait_metadata()`,
  `write_causal_variants()`, `write_phenotype_data()`) and corresponding Parquet
  schemas in `R/database.R`.
- **`DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_TRAIT_DATA`** Nextflow module (`modules/db_migration/write_trait_data/`);
  uses `.merge()` to align `GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES` output channels by emission
  order.
- Phenotype stored in `phenotypes/` is the pre-upscaling GCTA `.phen` output
  (before `check_vp.py`), making the ANOVA SS ratio for offline variance-explained
  scale-invariant.
- Unit tests in `test-genotype_storage.R` and `test-trait_storage.R`.
- Test fixture: `tests/fixtures/test_genotype_matrix.tsv` (5 markers × 3 strains)
  and `tests/fixtures/test_sims.phen` (3-strain GCTA `.phen` file).

#### Changed
- `var.exp` column removed from `mappings_schema()` and `prepare_mapping_data()`;
  it was always `NA` in the DB path. Raw data for offline estimation is now
  available in `traits/`, `causal_variants/`, and `phenotypes/`.
- All DB write functions default to `overwrite = TRUE` to handle non-atomic
  `arrow::write_parquet()` writes — a crashed task leaves a partial file;
  retry with `overwrite = TRUE` replaces it cleanly.
- Narrow glob `_markers\\.parquet$` used in `R/queries.R` to exclude genotype
  files from the `markers` DuckDB view (genotype files share the `marker_sets/`
  directory but have a different schema).
- Database integration workflow made the default path; legacy R assessment is
  now opt-in via `--legacy_assess`.


### PR #95 — Documentation Site (2026-02-18)

#### Added
- Quarto website in `docs/` published to GitHub Pages at
  `https://andersenlab.github.io/nemascan-sims-nf/`.
- One page per pipeline phase: Marker Set Generation, Trait Simulation, GWAS
  Mapping, QTL Detection & Assessment, Database Structure.
- Development guide covering local rendering, publishing, testing, HPC
  execution, and project structure.


### PR #93 — Cross-Validation Framework (2026-02-17)

#### Added
- `cross-validation.qmd` documentation page explaining concordance methodology.
- Cross-validation assessment script computing concordance scores (detection,
  designation, interval overlap) between the DB path and legacy R path.


## Phase 4 — DB-Path QTL Analysis & Assessment

### 2026-02-13

The database-integrated QTL detection and simulation assessment path was
introduced, enabling end-to-end analysis without exporting intermediate TSV files.

#### Added
- `DB_MIGRATION_ANALYZE_QTL` and `DB_MIGRATION_ASSESS_SIMS` Nextflow modules.
- `R/qtl_database.R` and `R/sim_performance.R` — database-native QTL grouping
  and simulation performance scoring.
- `R/assessment.R` — shared assessment logic sourced directly by
  `db_migration/assess_sims` and the legacy `Assess_Sims.R` scripts.
- Modular helpers split `R_GET_GCTA_INTERVALS` (legacy) into two processes:
  QTL identification and simulation performance assessment.
- Warning emitted when duplicated markers are detected during loading (expected
  only when migrating legacy processed-mapping TSV files).


## Phase 3 — Database Foundation

### Phases 1–3 (prior to 2026-02-13)

Core Parquet database infrastructure: marker-set ingestion, GWA statistics
storage, and DuckDB query layer.

#### Added
- `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET` module — writes `{pop}_{maf}_markers.parquet`
  and `marker_set_metadata.parquet`; emits `val true` on `done` to gate
  downstream GWA writes.
- `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GWA_TO_DB` module — Hive-partitioned GWA Parquet files
  under `mappings/population={pop}/mapping_id={id}/`; gated behind a
  `.collect()` barrier ensuring marker schemas exist first.
- `R/database.R`, `R/queries.R`, `R/analysis.R` — shared R library for
  Parquet I/O, DuckDB views, and log₁₀P computation.
- `safe_log10p()` in `R/analysis.R` — log₁₀P computed at query time, never
  stored; clamped at `LOG10P_MAX`.


## Early Pipeline Development (April–May 2025)

Initial Nextflow pipeline scaffold and iterative stabilization.

### PR #30 (2025-05-04) — Phenotype Simulation Fix
- Corrected `GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES` output file name and argument parsing.

### PR #29 (2025-05-04) — nQTL and Effect Size Inputs
- Added `nqtl` and `effect` as explicit pipeline parameters passed to the
  causal variant selection step.

### PR #25 (2025-05-01) — Mitochondrial Chromosome Handling
- Fixed null-handling for `mito_name` parameter so optional mitochondrial
  exclusion works correctly.

### PR #17 (2025-04-18) — Meta-tag Check
- Fixed `metaid` typo causing process metadata lookup failure.

### PR #8 (2025-04-11) — Replace `FIND` with `GET` Processes
- Renamed VCF-strain extraction process for consistency with NemaScan
  module naming conventions.

### PR #2 — Default Strain Set (2025-04-04)
- Added `data/test/` strain list; pipeline defaults to test strain set when
  `--strainfile` is not provided.

### PR #1 — Resource Allocation (2025-04-04)
- First draft of per-process CPU/RAM/time resource configuration.

### Initial Commit (2025-03-26)
- First working pipeline scaffold: marker set generation, GCTA simulation,
  GWAS mapping, and legacy R-based QTL detection and assessment.
